sourceMovie.title: Pain & Gain
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Pain & Gain for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Michael Bay keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Miss Congeniality
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pain & Gain clicked for you, Miss Congeniality (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pain & Gain?
faq.answer: Try Miss Congeniality, Lift, Mad Money—each shares DNA with Pain & Gain while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pain & Gain?
faq.answer: Pain & Gain (2013) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pain & Gain worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
